insight: For France, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. When they were pressing before a break, they tended to lose momentum, with their average change in momentum being -0.300 per minute, and they were unable to regain it after the break. However, when they weren't pressing before a break, they actually gained momentum, with an average change of +0.200 per minute, suggesting that the break allowed them to regroup and refocus. Overall, France were able to gain momentum in half of their hydration breaks, but the effectiveness of the break seemed to depend on their strategy and approach leading up to it.
